title: Food Intake, Meal Planning & Nutrition Timing
source: USDA, WHO, American Dietetic Association, NIH

chunks.title: Optimal Meal Frequency & Timing
chunks.content: Research consensus on meal timing: Eat within 1-2 hours of waking (breakfast kickstarts metabolism). Main meals: 3 per day, spaced 4-5 hours apart. Snacks: 1-2 small ones if meals are >5 hours apart (150-200 kcal each). Ideal meal times: Breakfast 7-9 AM, Lunch 12-1 PM, Dinner 6-7 PM. Eating window: Finish dinner 2-3 hours before bedtime (reduces acid reflux, improves sleep quality). Late-night eating (after 9 PM) is associated with weight gain and poor sleep. Source: American Dietetic Association, Chrononutrition research.

chunks.title: Healthy Portion Sizes (Hand Measurement Guide)
chunks.content: USDA-aligned portion guide using hand measurements: Protein (meat, fish): Palm of hand (~100g, ~3-4 oz). Carbs (rice, pasta): Cupped hand (~1 cup cooked, 200 kcal). Vegetables: Two open handfuls (~2 cups, unlimited). Fats (oils, nuts): Thumb tip (~1 tbsp, 100 kcal). Cheese: Two finger-widths (~30g, 100 kcal). Fruit: Fist-sized (~1 medium fruit, 60-80 kcal). Plate rule: Half plate vegetables, quarter protein, quarter carbs. Source: USDA MyPlate guidelines, British Nutrition Foundation.

chunks.title: Common Food Calorie Reference
chunks.content: Quick calorie reference for common foods: Rice (1 cup cooked): 210 kcal. Bread (1 slice): 80 kcal. Egg (1 large): 78 kcal. Chicken breast (100g): 165 kcal. Banana: 105 kcal. Apple: 95 kcal. Milk (1 cup): 150 kcal. Coffee (black): 2 kcal, with milk: 60 kcal. Pizza (1 slice): 285 kcal. Burger (regular): 350 kcal. Salad (with dressing): 200 kcal. Dal/lentils (1 cup): 230 kcal. Roti/chapati (1): 120 kcal. Idli (1): 58 kcal. Dosa (1 plain): 120 kcal. Biryani (1 plate): 450-650 kcal. Source: USDA FoodData Central, IFCT.

chunks.title: Water & Beverages with Meals
chunks.content: Drinking water 30 min before meals can reduce calorie intake by 75 kcal per meal (Virginia Tech, 2010). Small sips during meals are fine; large amounts may dilute digestive enzymes (no strong evidence against, but moderation is sensible). Best after-meal beverages: warm water or herbal tea (aids digestion). Avoid sugary drinks with meals (adds 150-250 kcal per serving). Coffee after meals may reduce iron absorption by up to 80% — wait 1 hour if anemic. Source: NIH, European Journal of Clinical Nutrition.
